reason: Response B reads more like Claude Haiku's typical style: flowing, warmly descriptive prose with clear paragraph organization around sensory dimensions (light, sound, smell, visual, mood), and a slightly more conventional, accessible tone. Response A has a more distinctive, deliberately fragmented rhetorical style with short punchy sentences, meta-commentary ("What rainy afternoons are not"), and a self-conscious structural flourish at the end ("That is the rainy afternoon") that feels more like a larger model performing minimalism. Haiku tends toward earnest, well-organized sensory description rather than that kind of self-aware prose architecture.
